rulings.Case Title: JAY B. BARNES et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 14, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Parker Hannifin Corporation¿s motion to strike punitive damages claim from plaintiff¿s first amended complaint is denied. Although plaintiffs did not file an opposition, one is not required. (See, e.g., Weil & Brown, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2010) at 7:122.) Plaintiffs¿ first amended complaint sufficiently pleads a claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAY B. BARNES et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 14, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Parker Hannifin Corporation¿s general demurrer to plaintiff¿s entire first amended complaint and special demurrers to the negligence, product liability and premises liability causes of action are overruled. Although plaintiffs did not file an opposition, one is not required. (See, e.g., Weil & Brown, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2010) at 7:122.) Plaintiffs¿ first amended complaint meets the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. It sufficiently pleads causes of action for negligence, product liability and premises liability. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL LYNN O'BRIEN VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 14, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Parker Hannifin Corporation¿s demurrer to plaintiff¿s complaint is off calendar. A dismissal of the entire action was filed November 4. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 14, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Parker Hannifin Corporation¿s motion to strike portions of plaintiff¿s complaint is off calendar. A dismissal of the entire action was filed November 4. = (503/TLJ)
